componentDescription: Accordion displays collapsible content sections. Use it for FAQs, progressive disclosure of settings, expandable detail rows, and any scenario where showing all content at once would overwhelm the user. Supports single and multi-expand modes.
features: Two border styles: BORDER (with dividers) and NO_BORDER (seamless)
Single expand (default) or multi-expand mode via isMultiple
Rich AccordionItem trigger: leftSlot, rightSlot, subtext, subtextSlot, triggerSlot
compositionPattern: Accordion wraps AccordionItem children. Each AccordionItem must have a unique 'value' string, a 'title', and 'children' for the expandable content. The Accordion component manages which items are open. Use defaultValue (string or string[]) for uncontrolled initial state.

usageExamples.title: FAQ accordion
usageExamples.code:
We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and PayPal.

Yes, you can cancel at any time from your account settings. Your plan remains active until the end of the billing period.

Yes, all new accounts get a 14-day free trial with full access.

usageExamples.title: Multi-expand settings accordion
usageExamples.description: Settings grouped in sections, multiple can be open simultaneously.
usageExamples.code:
<Accordion
  accordionType={AccordionType.NO_BORDER}
  isMultiple
  defaultValue={['notifications', 'privacy']}
>
  <AccordionItem
    value="notifications"
    title="Notifications"
    subtext="Email, SMS, and push settings"
    leftSlot={<BellIcon />}
  >
    <NotificationSettings />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem
    value="privacy"
    title="Privacy"
    subtext="Data sharing and visibility"
    leftSlot={<LockIcon />}
  >
    <PrivacySettings />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem
    value="billing"
    title="Billing"
    subtext="Payment methods and invoices"
    leftSlot={<CreditCardIcon />}
    isDisabled
  >
    <BillingSettings />
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

usageExamples.title: Controlled accordion
usageExamples.description: Accordion with external control over which item is expanded.
usageExamples.code:
<Accordion
  accordionType={AccordionType.BORDER}
  value={openItem}
  onValueChange={setOpenItem}
>
  <AccordionItem value="step-1" title="Step 1: Account details">
    <AccountForm onComplete={() => setOpenItem('step-2')} />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="step-2" title="Step 2: Payment">
    <PaymentForm onComplete={() => setOpenItem('step-3')} />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="step-3" title="Step 3: Review">
    <ReviewPanel />
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

accordionType.llmContext: BORDER adds visible borders/dividers between items, good for structured content like FAQs and forms. NO_BORDER removes separators for a seamless appearance, suitable for sidebar navigation or embedded settings panels.
accordionType.propDescription: Controls border/divider style between accordion items

isMultiple.llmContext: Set to true to allow multiple AccordionItems to be open simultaneously. Use for independent settings sections where opening one shouldn't close others. Leave false (default) for FAQs and step-by-step flows where only one section should be active.
isMultiple.propDescription: Allows multiple items to be open at the same time

defaultValue.llmContext: Set the initially open item(s) in uncontrolled mode. Pass a single value string for single-expand mode, or a string[] when isMultiple is true. Use to pre-expand the most relevant section.
defaultValue.propDescription: Initially open item value(s) in uncontrolled mode

value.llmContext: The currently open item value(s) in controlled mode. Pass a string for single-expand or string[] for multi-expand. Pair with onValueChange for full control.
value.propDescription: Currently open item value(s) in controlled mode

onValueChange.llmContext: Called with the new open value(s) when user expands or collapses an item. Update your state here. In single-expand mode, receives a string; in multi-expand mode, receives a string[].
onValueChange.propDescription: Called with new open value(s) when expansion state changes

value.llmContext: Required. Unique string identifier for this item within the Accordion. Used by the Accordion to track open/closed state. Use descriptive identifiers like 'billing-settings' or 'faq-payment'.
value.propDescription: Required. Unique identifier for this accordion item

title.llmContext: Required. The trigger text shown in the collapsed header. Should clearly describe the content inside. Use question form for FAQs, noun phrases for settings sections.
title.propDescription: Required. Header text shown when the item is collapsed

leftSlot.llmContext: React node rendered left of the title in the trigger. Use for section icons that help users scan and identify sections quickly.
leftSlot.propDescription: React node rendered to the left of the title

rightSlot.llmContext: React node rendered right of the title in the trigger (before the chevron). Use for status badges, counts, or secondary actions.
rightSlot.propDescription: React node rendered to the right of the title

subtext.llmContext: Secondary text shown below the title in the trigger. Use to describe what the section contains without requiring the user to expand it.
subtext.propDescription: Secondary descriptive text below the title

chevronPosition.llmContext: LEFT places the expand/collapse chevron on the left of the trigger. RIGHT places it on the right. RIGHT is the default and more conventional. Use LEFT for tree-style navigation patterns.
chevronPosition.propDescription: Position of the expand/collapse chevron icon

isDisabled.llmContext: Set to true to prevent this specific AccordionItem from being expanded. The item remains visible with a disabled appearance. Use for sections not available in the current plan or state.
isDisabled.propDescription: Prevents this accordion item from being expanded
